2021-06-01.title: Glum to Gleeful, Israeli Media React to Possible End of Netanyahu Era
2021-06-01.abstract: In Israel’s newspapers — as fractured as its electorate — the opinions on the possible fall of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu were as emphatic as they were varied.

2021-06-01.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-06-01.abstract: A new kind of pandemic grief.

2021-06-01.title: Australia’s softball players are among the first Olympic athletes to arrive in Japan.
2021-06-01.abstract: The team will be confined to one floor of a hotel as it prepares for the Tokyo Games, which are scheduled to begin July 23 despite a coronavirus outbreak and growing public opposition.

2021-06-01.title: China Wants More Babies. Some Men Choose Vasectomies.
2021-06-01.abstract: As the Chinese government tries to increase the country’s falling birthrate, some millennials are striving for a lifestyle commonly known as “Double Income, No Kids.”

2021-06-01.title: Have Three Children? No Way, Many Chinese Say.
2021-06-01.abstract: Intense workplace competition, inadequate child care and widespread job discrimination against pregnant women have made childbearing an unappealing prospect for many.

2021-06-02.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in June
2021-06-02.abstract: Our streaming picks for June, including ‘Sweet Tooth,’ ‘Good on Paper’ and ‘Eden'

2021-06-02.title: Fires Sink Iran’s Largest Warship and Ravage Big Refinery
2021-06-02.abstract: The double fire raised suspicions that Iran was once again a sabotage target, as diplomacy to resurrect the nuclear deal advanced.

2021-06-02.title: ‘Come On In, Boys’: A Wave of the Hand Sets Off Spain-Morocco Migrant Fight
2021-06-02.abstract: Spanish officials say Morocco increasingly sees migrants as currency for financial and political gains after it let up to 12,000 flood into a Spanish enclave in North Africa over two days.

2021-06-02.title: Portugal Is Riding a Boom in Bicycles
2021-06-02.abstract: To meet a jump in demand, the country’s bike-making industry is building new factories, hiring workers and dealing with parts shortages.

2021-06-03.title: Netanyahu Rivals Agree on Israeli Coalition to Oust Him
2021-06-03.abstract: Benjamin Netanyahu, Israel’s longest-serving leader, is on the verge of losing power as his opponents strike a deal that would replace him with an ultranationalist, Naftali Bennett.

2021-06-03.title: Israeli Opposition Reaches Coalition Agreement
2021-06-03.abstract: Israelis reacted with happiness and frustration to news that opposition parties had agreed to form a coalition government that would end Benjamin Netanyahu’s 12-year tenure as prime minister.

2021-06-03.title: About 10,000 volunteers for the Tokyo Olympics have quit, organizers say.
2021-06-03.abstract: The departures were the latest sign of opposition among the Japanese public to hosting the Games during the pandemic.

2021-06-03.title: Tasked to Fight Climate Change, a Secretive U.N. Agency Does the Opposite
2021-06-03.abstract: Behind closed doors, shipbuilders and miners can speak on behalf of governments while regulating an industry that pollutes as much as all of America’s coal plants.

2021-06-04.title: Fake Nudes and Real Threats: How Online Abuse Holds Back Women in Politics
2021-06-04.abstract: Female politicians face more personal online attacks than their male counterparts, researchers have found. That online abuse can escalate into violence.

2021-06-04.title: Subdued but Not Silenced, Hong Kong Tries to Remember Tiananmen Massacre
2021-06-04.abstract: Officials banned the annual June 4 vigil, as a new security law looms over commemorations of the 1989 crackdown. Hong Kongers wonder how long the memory will remain.

2021-06-04.title: New Zealand Court Clears Way for Murder Suspect’s Extradition to China
2021-06-04.abstract: The Supreme Court insisted on assurances from Beijing that the man, accused of killing a Chinese woman in 2009, would not be tortured and would get a fair trial.

2021-06-04.title: The Guardians of Australia’s Memory Try Crowdfunding
2021-06-04.abstract: The National Archives’ funding crisis has sparked a discussion about whether we undervalue our country’s history.

2021-06-05.title: Florida will no longer publish daily coronavirus reports.
2021-06-05.abstract: The Florida Department of Health will no longer update its Covid-19 dashboard and will suspend daily case and vaccine reports, the governor’s office confirmed on Friday. Officials will instead post weekly updates, becoming the first U.S. state to move to such an infrequent publishing schedule.

2021-06-05.title: Election in East Germany Will Test the Far Right’s Power
2021-06-05.abstract: Voting on Sunday may hint at how strong the Alternative for Germany party is in the east, and what that means for national elections in September.

2021-06-05.title: As Vaccines Turn Pandemic’s Tide, U.S. and Europe Diverge on Path Forward
2021-06-05.abstract: The split is particularly stark in Britain, which is facing the spread of a new variant, while America has essentially lifted all rules for people who are vaccinated.

2021-06-05.title: After Lavish Nights of Clubbing in Bangkok, a Covid Outbreak
2021-06-05.abstract: Thailand went for months without a single confirmed case of local transmission, but the epidemic has now radiated from nightclubs to areas where social distancing is all but impossible.

2021-06-05.title: Olympics Gave Hope to Japan’s L.G.B.T.Q. Activists. But Old Prejudices Die Hard.
2021-06-05.abstract: Legislation labeling discrimination “unacceptable” has been blocked by conservative lawmakers, showing how far the country has to go to fulfill the goal of equality enshrined in the Olympic charter.

2021-06-06.title: Biden Is Embracing Europe, but Then What? NATO and the E.U. Have Concerns.
2021-06-06.abstract: After the Trump trauma, Europe will welcome Biden’s warm words, but tough topics remain, from China and Russia to trade disputes, Covid-19 vaccine diplomacy and transitions to carbon-neutral economies.

2021-06-06.title: U.S. Aid to Central America Hasn’t Slowed Migration. Can Kamala Harris?
2021-06-06.abstract: A stark reality faces the vice president as she visits the region: Expanded aid programs have failed to stem migration.

2021-06-06.title: ‘Fear and Vigilance’: Israel’s Ultra-Orthodox Face a Fall from Power
2021-06-06.abstract: A new coalition that aims to remove Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu from office could also usher in a more liberal civil rights agenda.

2021-06-06.title: Left and Right Clash in Peru Election, With an Economic Model at Stake
2021-06-06.abstract: A leftist former schoolteacher with no governing experience and the right-wing daughter of a jailed ex-president faced off for president on Sunday.

2021-06-06.title: The rush to vaccinate the world stalls as funds and doses fall short.
2021-06-06.abstract: Billion-dollar pledges — and the news that the United States will distribute an initial 25 million doses  — will do little to curb the explosive outbreaks in poor countries, experts said

2021-06-07.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-06-07.abstract: A warning about incitement in Israel.

2021-06-07.title: 2 Trains Collide in Pakistan, Killing at Least 35
2021-06-07.abstract: Most of the passengers on an express train were asleep when it derailed and its cars fell across the track into the path of another.

2021-06-07.title: Rome Gets Its First Pizza Vending Machine. Will Romans Bite?
2021-06-07.abstract: In a city with no shortage of pizza — or self-appointed food critics — an entrepreneur is betting on diners with a sense of adventure.

2021-06-07.title: From Doomsday Preppers to Doomsday Plotters
2021-06-07.abstract: Far-right movements have long dreamed of a moment that ends society as we’ve known it. Now, experts say, so-called accelerationist thinking is proliferating in ways that could destabilize democracy.

2021-06-07.title: The American South could see a summer Covid surge as vaccinations lag.
2021-06-07.abstract: In 15 states, including Arkansas, the Carolinas, Georgia and Louisiana, about half of adults or fewer have received a vaccine dose.

2021-06-08.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-06-08.abstract: A tense summer for Northern Ireland.

2021-06-08.title: China’s Leader Wants a ‘Lovable’ Country. That Doesn’t Mean He’s Making Nice.
2021-06-08.abstract: Xi Jinping’s remarks to top Communist Party leaders suggest Beijing will focus on courting allies rather than easing its rhetoric against the U.S. and Europe.

2021-06-08.title: The Mayor, the Teacher and a Fight Over a ‘Lost Territory’ of France
2021-06-08.abstract: A battle erupted after a high-school teacher said that Islamists had taken over the city of Trappes, a view vigorously countered by the mayor, sharpening a national debate over French identity.

2021-06-08.title: Israel’s Parliament to Vote on New Government on Sunday
2021-06-08.abstract: If lawmakers approve a fragile new coalition government, Israel will be led by someone other than Benjamin Netanyahu for the first time in 12 years.

2021-06-08.title: The Criminals Thought the Devices Were Secure. But the Seller Was the F.B.I.
2021-06-08.abstract: Global law enforcement officials revealed a three-year operation in which they said they had intercepted over 20 million messages. Hundreds of arrests were made in more than a dozen countries.

2021-06-09.title: Attack in Afghanistan Kills 10 From Charity That Clears Land Mines
2021-06-09.abstract: The government blamed the Taliban for the attack on the British-American charity, the HALO Trust. The militant group denied any responsibility.

2021-06-09.title: More than a year into the pandemic, many Americans are still getting terrible sleep.
2021-06-09.abstract: In a survey of thousands of adults last summer, the American Academy of Sleep Medicine found that 20 percent of Americans said they had trouble sleeping because of the pandemic.

2021-06-09.title: A slow reopening continues in France, and other news from around the world.
2021-06-09.abstract: France, which has seen its coronavirus situation improve in recent weeks, is beginning its third phase of a gradual reopening. Indoor service in bars, cafes and restaurants will be allowed to resume, although at limited capacity, and terraces will be able to operate at 100 percent capacity.

2021-06-09.title: Wuhan animal markets sold dozens of species that can carry pathogens that infect humans, a study found.
2021-06-09.abstract: The study sheds new light on the possible role of the wildlife trade in the emergence of the coronavirus.

2021-06-10.title: California’s vaccinated residents can go mostly mask-free as of June 15.
2021-06-10.abstract: The shift will coincide with the much-anticipated lifting of remaining business capacity restrictions in the state, where life has been disrupted for some 15 months.

2021-06-10.title: A Nevada man is charged in the theft of more than 500 blank vaccine cards in Los Angeles.
2021-06-10.abstract: The police and prosecutors determined that the value of the cards was “at least $15 apiece if illegally sold.”

2021-06-10.title: Dreaming of ‘Global Britain,’ Johnson Is Attacked for Cutting Foreign Aid
2021-06-10.abstract: As Prime Minister Boris Johnson prepares to host the Group of 7 meeting, he faces a battle over efforts to cut foreign aid by a third to help make up for the costs of fighting the coronavirus.

2021-06-10.title: Addressing the global vaccine shortage, Biden cites ‘our humanitarian obligation, to save as many lives as we can.’
2021-06-10.abstract: “When we see people hurting and suffering anywhere around the world, we seek to help any way we can,” Mr. Biden said in a speech in England, ahead of the meeting of the Group of 7 wealthy democracies.

2021-06-11.title: What is the G7 summit, and why does it matter?
2021-06-11.abstract: The nations in the club are the world’s wealthiest large democracies, close allies and major trading partners that account for about half of the global economy.

2021-06-11.title: Kim Jong-un Calls K-Pop a ‘Vicious Cancer’ in the New Culture War
2021-06-11.abstract: South Korean music, movies and dramas are winning the hearts of young North Koreans. Their influence is seen as a threat to Mr. Kim’s grip on society.

2021-06-11.title: A cruise ship required all aboard to be vaccinated. Then two passengers tested positive for the virus.
2021-06-11.abstract: As large gatherings resume in the U.S., infections on a cruise ship and at a Bitcoin conference show the challenges of reopening.

2021-06-11.title: America May Be ‘Back’ in Europe, but How Much Has Really Changed?
2021-06-11.abstract: Despite the feel-good imagery expected to be on display at the Group of 7 meeting, many Europeans suspect that President Biden is little more interested in give-and-take than was his predecessor.

2021-06-12.title: The G7 leaders get down to business, taking on climate change and the pandemic.
2021-06-12.abstract: The Group of 7 leaders reach the heart of their annual summit meeting on Saturday, with the pandemic, climate change, China and Russia likely to be on the agenda at their gathering in Cornwall, England.

2021-06-12.title: In a Crisis, India’s Modi Could Always Change the Narrative. Then Came Covid.
2021-06-12.abstract: His efforts to squelch dissent and to accentuate the positive may not be able to counter widespread anger over his government’s stumbling response, undermining a key part of his strategy for wielding power.

2021-06-12.title: Angela Merkel, Anchor of European Stability, Stays Focused at Her Final G7
2021-06-12.abstract: The German chancellor, known for her commitment to compromise, is eager to revive deal-making on multilateral policy, joining the world’s top democratic leaders one last time. Can she be replaced?

2021-06-12.title: In China’s latest outbreak, doctors say the infected get sicker, faster.
2021-06-12.abstract: Four-fifths of symptomatic cases develop fevers, more than in earlier outbreaks. The findings match trends in other countries where the Delta variant is spreading.

2021-06-12.title: A thrilling tennis match won fans an exception to France’s curfew.
2021-06-12.abstract: French officials delivered one of the great moments of sports in the pandemic on Friday night, as the clock crept toward an 11 p.m. curfew in Paris that seemed destined to clear the stands during an epic French Open men’s tennis semifinal between Rafael Nadal of Spain and Novak Djokovic of Serbia.

2021-06-13.title: F.D.A. details failures at a Baltimore plant that led to unusable vaccine doses.
2021-06-13.abstract: The F.D.A. advised Johnson & Johnson on Friday that it should throw out the equivalent of 60 million doses produced at the Baltimore plant.

2021-06-13.title: Why the Mexico City Metro Collapsed
2021-06-13.abstract: A Times investigation shows the serious construction flaws and political pressure behind a tragedy that threatens two of Mexico’s most prominent figures.

2021-06-13.title: Explosion at Produce Market Kills at Least 12 in China
2021-06-13.abstract: The cause of the blast, which took place in the central city of Shiyan, was still being investigated.

2021-06-13.title: It’s Angela Merkel’s last G7 summit. What will the group be like without her?
2021-06-13.abstract: Angela Merkel, who plans to step down as Germany’s chancellor after the country holds elections in September, is attending her final Group of 7 summit this weekend.

2021-06-13.title: The Knesset is voting now on Bennett’s coalition.
2021-06-13.abstract: If it is approved, a new government will remove Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and end two years of political gridlock.

2021-06-14.title: Netanyahu Ousted as Israeli Parliament Votes in New Government
2021-06-14.abstract: An unlikely coalition prevailed against the country’s longest-serving leader. Now it must get its disparate factions to work together.

2021-06-14.title: England’s plan to reopen on June 21 is postponed by four weeks.
2021-06-14.abstract: Restaurants and pubs in England, while open, will still have to observe social distancing rules indoors, limiting capacity, and nightclubs and theaters will remain firmly shuttered. The decision, which will be reviewed in two weeks, sent a warning to the world that even well-vaccinated nations remain at risk.

2021-06-14.title: Hollywood Take on Christchurch Massacre Provokes Anger in New Zealand
2021-06-14.abstract: Members of the Muslim community denounced as “white saviorism” the director’s decision to focus on the response by Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern.

2021-06-14.title: What to watch for in today’s NATO summit.
2021-06-14.abstract: Monday’s NATO summit meeting of 30 leaders is short, with one 2.5-hour session after an opening ceremony, leaving just five minutes for each leader to speak.

2021-06-15.title: Asian countries that tamed the virus find that reopening is more difficult.
2021-06-15.abstract: All across the Asia-Pacific region, the countries that led the world in containing the coronavirus are languishing in the race to put it behind them.

2021-06-15.title: What are the roadblocks to a ‘vaccine passport’ in America?
2021-06-15.abstract: With all American adults now eligible for Covid-19 vaccines, and businesses and international borders reopening, a fierce debate has kicked off across the United States over whether a digital health certificate — often and somewhat misleadingly called a “vaccine passport” — should be required to prove immunization status.

2021-06-15.title: Why Asia, the Pandemic Champion, Remains Miles Away From the Finish Line
2021-06-15.abstract: While the United States edges toward normalcy, countries like Japan, South Korea and Australia are still facing months of uncertainty and isolation as their vaccination campaigns just start to gain steam.

2021-06-15.title: England’s delayed reopening is a blow to culture and nightlife.
2021-06-15.abstract: Theaters and nightclub owners are having to hastily rearrange and cancel events after the government decided to maintain some restrictions.

2021-06-16.title: Where Did Hideki Tojo’s Body Go After His Execution? A Mystery Is Solved.
2021-06-16.abstract: The location of the remains of the wartime Japanese prime minister had been a puzzle. Now, documents reveal that U.S. forces secretly scattered his ashes into the Pacific Ocean.

2021-06-16.title: North Korea Is Facing a ‘Tense’ Food Shortage
2021-06-16.abstract: Kim Jong-un said addressing food insecurity was “a top priority” in the country, where millions died in a famine during the ’90s.

2021-06-16.title: Zimbabwe Releases Local Reporter Working for The New York Times
2021-06-16.abstract: The government said that it would not oppose bail for Jeffrey Moyo, a freelance journalist in the capital, Harare, adding that there was evidence to show he had believed he was acting legally.

2021-06-16.title: Biden and Putin say the talks went well, but divisions remain on issues like cyberattacks and human rights.
2021-06-16.abstract: Mr. Biden and Mr. Putin emerged from their first in-person summit offering broad claims of good will.

2021-06-16.title: Biden raised issues of human rights and digital disruption.
2021-06-16.abstract: The American leader aimed to look strong on security while also not making too many waves.

2021-06-17.title: Watch Live: China Sends Astronauts to Space Station
2021-06-17.abstract: China launches the Shenzhou-12 spacecraft carrying three astronauts from Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center to the partially constructed Tianhe space station where they will stay for three months.

2021-06-17.title: Hong Kong Cracks Down on a Pro-Democracy Newspaper
2021-06-17.abstract: Police arrested the top editors of Apple Daily, froze its assets and raided its newsroom, in a sharp escalation of the government’s campaign against dissent.

2021-06-17.title: ‘Springsteen on Broadway’ is returning, but not for guests who haven’t had F.D.A.-authorized vaccines.
2021-06-17.abstract: The venue, New York’s St. James Theatre, is making no exceptions for people who are unable or unwilling to be vaccinated.

2021-06-17.title: Your Thursday Briefing
2021-06-17.abstract: Biden and Putin meet in Geneva.

2021-06-17.title: After Putin Meeting, a Biden Trait Shows Itself Again: Stubborn Optimism
2021-06-17.abstract: The president’s insistence on an “optimistic face” could open him up to criticism. But his allies insist it’s an essential ingredient to making progress.

2021-06-18.title: Kim Jong-un Says He’s Ready for ‘Dialogue and Confrontation’ With Biden
2021-06-18.abstract: During a Workers’ Party meeting, the North Korean leader reviewed the new U.S. policy on his country and ordered “counteraction,” state news media reported.

2021-06-18.title: What I Learned About Australia’s Lackluster Vaccination Effort From the Latest Covid ‘Hot Spot’ in Sydney
2021-06-18.abstract: Australia is great at outbreak response but something is lacking when it comes to the country’s vaccine rollout.

2021-06-18.title: The Maldives Lured Tourists Back. Now It Needs Nurses.
2021-06-18.abstract: The island nation kept Covid cases low, and its resorts open, for much of the pandemic. But a recent surge exposed its overreliance on expatriate health workers.

2021-06-18.title: The Power of Money: How Autocrats Use London to Strike Foes Worldwide
2021-06-18.abstract: English courtrooms have become a battleground — and a source of powerful weapons — in fierce disputes between the tycoons and the politicians of the post-Soviet world.

2021-06-18.title: Portugal orders Lisbon into a weekend lockdown as the Delta variant spreads.
2021-06-18.abstract: The decision came after the country registered this week its highest number of new cases since March, jumping by more than 1,300 in the past 24 hours.

2021-06-19.title: U.N. Condemns Myanmar Military Over Coup
2021-06-19.abstract: The United Nations General Assembly adopted a resolution on Friday rebuking the Myanmar military and demanding an end to its five-month-old coup.

2021-06-19.title: Iranian Hard-Liner Ebrahim Raisi Wins Presidential Vote
2021-06-19.abstract: The government announced his victory on Saturday, a day after a vote that many Iranians skipped, viewing it as rigged.

2021-06-19.title: Brazil, Besieged by Covid, Now Faces a Severe Drought
2021-06-19.abstract: Brazilians are paying more for electricity, dealing with the possibility of water rationing and expecting a destructive fire season in the Amazon in the worst dry spell in at least 90 years.

2021-06-19.title: Inside the ‘Deadly Serious’ World of E-Sports in South Korea
2021-06-19.abstract: Each year, thousands of young South Koreans compete to join pro e-sports teams, but only a few make the cut​. An American company in Seoul wants to help more young gamers find jobs.

2021-06-19.title: A Roadblock for Iran’s President-Elect: He’s on the U.S. Sanctions List
2021-06-19.abstract: Ebrahim Raisi, a close ally of the country’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been accused of grave human rights violations.

2021-06-20.title: French Far Right Is Dealt a Setback in First Round of Regional Elections
2021-06-20.abstract: A center-right party, Les Républicains, led the vote Sunday, while Marine Le Pen’s National Rally, which had high hopes, pulled ahead in only one region.

2021-06-20.title: Iran’s System Keeps Its Grip, Despite the Chaos (or Because of It)
2021-06-20.abstract: Governments rooted in revolution like Tehran’s have proved to be among the world’s most stable, even drawing strength from crises.

2021-06-20.title: In the Wake of India’s Covid Crisis, a ‘Black Fungus’ Epidemic Follows
2021-06-20.abstract: The deadly disease has sickened former coronavirus patients across the country. Doctors believe that hospitals desperate to keep Covid patients alive made choices that left them vulnerable.

2021-06-20.title: In a Muffled Hong Kong, Bookstores Offer Freedom of Thought
2021-06-20.abstract: Some independent shops flout the new limits on free expression. Others try to come to terms with them. For readers, they offer a sense of connection in a changed city.

2021-06-20.title: John Bercow, Central Figure in Brexit Drama as U.K. Speaker, Switches to Labour
2021-06-20.abstract: Mr. Bercow was a lawmaker with the governing Conservatives before being elected speaker, a role in which he irked the governments of both Theresa May and Boris Johnson.

2021-06-21.title: From Nobel Hero to Driver of War, Ethiopia’s Leader Faces Voters
2021-06-21.abstract: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed plunged Ethiopia into a war in the Tigray region that spawned atrocities and famine. On Monday, his country goes to the polls.

2021-06-21.title: The Tokyo Olympics will allow spectators who live in Japan, but with restrictions.
2021-06-21.abstract: The decision indicates a growing certainty that the Games will go ahead, despite months of concern that they could become a superspreader event.

2021-06-21.title: The pandemic stimulus in the U.S. was front-loaded. That could mean a bumpy year.
2021-06-21.abstract: The $1.9 trillion American Rescue Plan enacted in March, as well as a $900 billion pandemic aid package passed in December, are heavily front-loaded. They were set up to get money out the door fast.

2021-06-21.title: Belarus Faces Expanded E.U. and U.S. Sanctions, Targeting Economy
2021-06-21.abstract: In a new effort to punish the Lukashenko government for repression, Western countries jointly focused on industries crucial to the country, including potash and finance.

2021-06-21.title: Armenia’s Governing Party Wins Election Seen as Vote on Peace Deal
2021-06-21.abstract: The party of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan won a snap parliamentary election in which rivals had talked of renegotiating his unpopular settlement with Azerbaijan.

2021-06-22.title: The virus is ravaging Colombia, where the death toll surpassed 100,000.
2021-06-22.abstract: The surge in the country has been steadily worsening since spring and less than 10 percent of its population is fully vaccinated.

2021-06-22.title: First They Lace Up Their Skates. Then They Fight Terrorism.
2021-06-22.abstract: The police in Karachi, Pakistan, put 20 officers on in-line skates for counterterrorism patrols and to soften the force’s image. Critics call it a publicity stunt.

2021-06-22.title: Attacked and Vulnerable, Some Afghans Are Forming Their Own Armies
2021-06-22.abstract: With U.S. troops leaving, the Taliban advancing, and a steady collapse of security force bases and outposts, the Hazara and other ethnic groups in Afghanistan are raising militias.

2021-06-22.title: U.N. Says Great Barrier Reef Is ‘in Danger.’ Australia Bitterly Disagrees.
2021-06-22.abstract: UNESCO has called on the government to quickly mitigate the effects of climate change on the natural wonder and tourist destination, but officials say they are already doing plenty.

2021-06-22.title: Many parts of the U.S. needed persuading to get vaccinated. Not South Texas.
2021-06-22.abstract: While officials across the United States have offered free beer, concert tickets and millions of dollars in lottery winnings to encourage vaccinations, residents of the Rio Grande Valley in South Texas have needed little prodding. Exposure to death and disease has been enough incentive.

2021-06-23.title: Protester Who Crashed Into Police Faces Hong Kong’s First Security Law Trial
2021-06-23.abstract: Tong Ying-kit, who hit officers while riding a motorcycle with a protest flag, is accused of terrorism and inciting secession. He could be sentenced to life in prison.

2021-06-23.title: The pandemic affected mental health and college plans for U.S. high schoolers.
2021-06-23.abstract: Nearly 80 percent of American high school juniors and seniors say the coronavirus pandemic has affected their plans after graduation, and 72 percent of 13- to 19-year-olds have struggled with their mental health, a new survey shows.

2021-06-23.title: A Month Before the Olympics, How Is Japan Faring With Covid?
2021-06-23.abstract: In the past month, cases have decreased and vaccination has ramped up dramatically — but with less than one-fifth of the country even partially vaccinated, many residents remain worried about hosting the Games.

2021-06-23.title: A Joyful Surprise at Japan’s Oldest Zoo: The Birth of Twin Pandas
2021-06-23.abstract: After a four-year hiatus, two 15-year-old giant pandas at last found the mood and added a new draw for one of Tokyo’s biggest tourist attractions.

2021-06-23.title: ‘Forbidden Fruit’: Apple Daily, Pro-Democracy Newspaper in Hong Kong, Is Forced to Close
2021-06-23.abstract: The police also arrested an editorial writer as part of an expanding national security investigation into the newspaper that has raised concerns about free speech.

2021-06-24.title: Iran Atomic Agency Says It Thwarted Attack on a Facility
2021-06-24.abstract: The attack was said to have been carried out by a small drone against a manufacturing center used in the production of centrifuges.

2021-06-24.title: The White House plans to send 3 million doses of Johnson & Johnson’s vaccine to Brazil on Thursday.
2021-06-24.abstract: The shipment is part of President Biden’s pledge to deliver 80 million doses overseas by the end of June.

2021-06-24.title: Benigno S. Aquino III, 61, Former President of the Philippines, Dies
2021-06-24.abstract: A scion of the country’s most prominent pro-democracy political family, he was celebrated early in his administration for stabilizing the country’s faltering economy.

2021-06-24.title: Nearly 900 Secret Service employees were infected with the virus, a watchdog group finds.
2021-06-24.abstract: The Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington criticized former President Trump for holding large campaign rallies that it said exposed agents to the virus.

2021-06-24.title: San Francisco will require all city employees to be vaccinated.
2021-06-24.abstract: The city is one of the largest U.S. municipalities to impose a Covid vaccine mandate for public workers, who could lose their jobs if they decline.

2021-06-25.title: Gibraltar Votes to Ease Abortion Restrictions
2021-06-25.abstract: Voters decided by a wide margin to lift a near-ban on the procedure, which had been one of the strictest such laws in Europe.

2021-06-25.title: Unmarked Graves of Indigenous Children Are Found in Canada
2021-06-25.abstract: Leaders of an Indigenous group said on Thursday that they had discovered the remains of as many as 751 people, mainly Indigenous children, at the site of a former school in Saskatchewan.

2021-06-25.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-06-25.abstract: Dozens missing after Florida building collapse.

2021-06-25.title: Confident in Its Impunity, the Myanmar Junta Ignores Diplomacy
2021-06-25.abstract: The generals who seized power five months ago have shown no inclination to heed international pleas to reverse themselves, even as Myanmar slides into a failed state.

2021-06-25.title: Why Are Australian Homes So Cold?
2021-06-25.abstract: Another issue the pandemic has thrown into sharp relief: our houses’ lack of insulation.

2021-06-26.title: Royal Drama Unfolds in Court, but a Prince at the Center of It Is Absent
2021-06-26.abstract: Two prominent figures are on trial in Jordan accused of plotting to destabilize the kingdom. One is an adviser to Saudi Arabia’s leader, and the Saudis have been trying to disrupt the trial.

2021-06-26.title: 1+1=4? Latin America Confronts a Pandemic Education Crisis.
2021-06-26.abstract: With economies reeling and millions cut off from the classroom, Latin America’s students are leaving school in alarming numbers, experts say.

2021-06-26.title: In France’s Military, Muslims Find a Tolerance That Is Elusive Elsewhere
2021-06-26.abstract: In a nation wary of the growth of Islam, France’s military has embraced and integrated its Muslim soldiers by facilitating their religious practice.

2021-06-26.title: Sydney, Australia, enters full lockdown for the first time in more than a year to fight the Delta variant.
2021-06-26.abstract: A cluster that began with an airport limousine driver has jumped to nearly 100 cases, with dozens more expected over the coming days.

2021-06-26.title: As Sudan Stumbles Toward Peace, a Standoff Over Corpses Is the Latest Obstacle
2021-06-26.abstract: The Sudanese deposed a dictator, but some fear true stability can never come until the truth is revealed about who killed more than a hundred protesters.

2021-06-27.title: Dozens Came Down With Covid-19 on Everest. Nepal Says It Never Happened.
2021-06-27.abstract: Climbers posted firsthand accounts of being infected, but officials in Nepal, which relies on tourism revenue, dismissed them as rumors.

2021-06-27.title: Royal Yacht or Fishing Trawler? Either Way, Even the Royal Family Isn’t Interested.
2021-06-27.abstract: For Prime Minister Boris Johnson, the proposed $280 million vessel would serve as a symbol of Global Britain. To critics, it’s an ill-conceived boondoggle.

2021-06-27.title: Pope Sends More Mixed Messages on L.G.B.T.Q. Rights
2021-06-27.abstract: An encouraging note from Pope Francis capped an especially disorienting week on the Vatican’s stance toward gay rights.

2021-06-27.title: Millions in Brazil are missing their second vaccine dose, adding to the burden of a hard-hit nation.
2021-06-27.abstract: Nearly 16 percent of those eligible for a second dose had not returned for them as of June 1.

2021-06-27.title: ‘A Fiasco’: Redesign Around Mosul Landmark Prompts Outcry
2021-06-27.abstract: The restoration is seen as essential to the idea that the Iraqi city has moved on from ISIS. But some critics say the plan betrays Iraq’s heritage.

2021-06-28.title: Migrants on U.S. Border Share Journey in Self-Portraits
2021-06-28.abstract: The Times photographer Adam Ferguson worked with migrants in Mexico to create a series of self-portraits as they waited to cross the border into the United States.

2021-06-28.title: Crossing the Red Line: Behind China’s Takeover of Hong Kong
2021-06-28.abstract: One year ago, the city’s freedoms were curtailed with breathtaking speed. But the clampdown was years in the making, and many signals were missed.

2021-06-28.title: A third dose of the AstraZeneca vaccine is found to boost immune response.
2021-06-28.abstract: A third shot of the vaccine lifted immune response in volunteers who had already received two.

2021-06-28.title: Migrant workers flee Bangladesh’s capital ahead of a new lockdown.
2021-06-28.abstract: Much of the public transportation system is already closing or closed, with new measures to control the spread of the virus scheduled to come fully into force on Thursday.

2021-06-28.title: Iraq Is Caught in the Middle as U.S. and Iran Spar on Its Soil
2021-06-28.abstract: Baghdad condemned American airstrikes on Iranian-controlled militias that have been harrying U.S. operations in Iraq, fearing an escalation.

2021-06-29.title: International Court Rules in Favor of Trans Rights in Honduras
2021-06-29.abstract: The landmark decision from the Inter-American Court of Human Rights, born out of a brutal murder, could have far-reaching implications for L.G.B.T.Q. rights across Latin America.

2021-06-29.title: Hong Kong bars passenger flights from Britain.
2021-06-29.abstract: Hong Kong cited the spread of the more virulent Delta coronavirus variant in the United Kingdom, which will be reclassified as “extremely high-risk.”

2021-06-29.title: ‘A Form of Brainwashing’: China Remakes Hong Kong
2021-06-29.abstract: Neighbors are urged to report on one another. Children are taught to look for traitors. Officials are pressed to pledge their loyalty.

2021-06-29.title: South African Court Orders Arrest of Ex-President Jacob Zuma for Contempt
2021-06-29.abstract: Mr. Zuma, whose tenure was tainted by financial scandals, was a comrade of Nelson Mandela and a dominant figure for decades in the governing African National Congress party.

2021-06-29.title: W.H.O. officials, concerned about the Delta variant, urge vaccinated people to keep wearing masks.
2021-06-29.abstract: Los Angeles County strongly recommended on Monday that everyone wear masks indoors as a precaution against the Delta variant.

2021-06-30.title: North Korea reports a ‘great crisis’ in its virus response.
2021-06-30.abstract: Kim Jong-un called a party meeting to criticize “ignorance” and reshuffle the leadership, but state media did not say if there had been an outbreak.

2021-06-30.title: Expected to Be Demure, Japan’s Girls Face Steep Hurdles to Athletic Dreams
2021-06-30.abstract: The Tokyo Olympics offer a chance to crown a new set of heroes to inspire budding female athletes. But once the spotlight dims, Japan’s rigid gender norms will still limit opportunities.

2021-06-30.title: W.H.O. Says China Has Eliminated Malaria
2021-06-30.abstract: The eradication of malaria in the world’s most populous nation offers lessons in how innovative treatments and aggressive tracking can keep the disease in check.

2021-06-30.title: As the Virus Rages, Putin Pushes Russians to Get a (Russian) Vaccine
2021-06-30.abstract: In his annual call-in show, President Vladimir V. Putin struck a changed tone after previously having cast his country’s handling of Covid-19 as a success story.

2021-06-30.title: Australian officials are divided over the AstraZeneca vaccine.
2021-06-30.abstract: With the Delta variant spreading, Prime Minister Scott Morrison is making the AstraZeneca vaccine available to younger people who want it. Some state officials say it’s the wrong call.
